questions.prompt: What's the relationship between Products and Prices in Stripe?

choices.text: A Product is the abstract thing you sell ('Pro Plan'); a Price is a specific pricing for it ($20/mo, $200/year, $0.10/unit). One Product can have many Prices. This separation lets you offer multiple pricing options for the same offering and change pricing without losing the product identity.

questions.explanation: Products and Prices are separate by design. The Product is what you sell; Prices are how you sell it. One Pro Plan product can have a $20/mo Price, a $200/year Price, and a Price for enterprise. Subscriptions reference Prices, not Products directly.

questions.prompt: A payment fails. You need to figure out which user to notify but you don't have a clean way to link Stripe back to your DB. Best practice you should have followed?

choices.text: Use email as the join key.

Put internal IDs in the `metadata` field on every Stripe object — Customer, PaymentIntent, Subscription, Invoice. E.g. `metadata: { user_id: 'u_123', plan_tier: 'pro' }`. Then any webhook or API response includes your internal ID for fast lookup. Don't put secrets in metadata; it's visible in dashboard and logs.

questions.explanation: Metadata is Stripe's pressure-relief valve for app-specific data. Put your user IDs and any context you'll need later in every relevant Stripe object. Reconciling without metadata is painful; with it, it's a single lookup. Don't put secrets — visible in dashboard.
